mechanism_narrative: The level of industrial production (INDPRO) acts as a real-time proxy for aggregate supply and economic momentum. Sustained increases in INDPRO indicate expanding output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO level and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal loses significance in a multivariate regression controlling for other macro factors.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a threshold of 4.5% signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the 20-day forward return distribution is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 4.5% and the CPI level is above 320, it indicates a stagflationary environment where the Fed's tightening fails to contain inflation while choking growth. This joint condition depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 320, the 60-day forward SPX return is not negative on average, or the Rank IC for the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL) is below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals consumer demand strength. In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE < 4.5%), positive retail sales growth translates into immediate positive S&P 500 returns as the market rewards robust consumption. The signal is strongest in this regime and weakens or reverses when unemployment is high.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 4.5%, the Rank IC of RSAFS growth with 5-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the direction of the relationship flips to negative in a statistically significant way.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,400 thousand units per month signal a robust housing market that stimulates related industries and consumer wealth. This spillover boosts economic growth and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1400, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the signal has a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high payroll employment growth (PAYEMS > +200k per month) and very low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) indicate an overheated labor market that fuels wage inflation and compresses profit margins. This interactive effect pressures equity valuations downward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where PAYEMS growth > 200k and UNRATE < 4%, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative on average, or the interaction term's predictive power (Rank IC) is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 1% signals a manufacturing contraction that typically precedes a broader economic slowdown. This supply-side weakness reduces earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After a 3-month INDPRO decline exceeding 1%, the 60-day forward SPX return is not negative in at least 60% of such events, or the average return is not significantly less than zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth is the broadest measure of economic output. A positive quarterly GDP print confirms economic expansion, which should support corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors adjust to the growth signal.
failure_condition: The Rank IC between GDPC1 quarterly growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal is not robust to revision risk (i.e., using revised vs. vintage data yields conflicting signs).
